name: bean
description: A documentation-only Codex plugin adding one explicit /bean skill: run a task as a recursive convergence loop — investigate, record evidence in a ledger, compile, revise beliefs, and loop until converged.

interface.shortDescription: Explicit /bean recursive convergence loop for careful tasks
interface.longDescription: bean is a provider-neutral skill that runs a task as a recursive convergence loop: investigate the most decisive open question, record what you learn as typed evidence in a ledger, compile to score what is still weak or contradictory, revise beliefs the evidence overturns, and loop until it converges. On Codex it runs on a minimal built-in ledger (bean-stalk.md) with hand-checked convergence. Documentation-only — no tools, servers, hooks, network calls, dependencies, or telemetry. Modeled on how the Fable model worked; a loop discipline, not a capability transplant.
defaultPrompt: Use /bean to investigate, record evidence, and loop until the answer converges.
Use /bean to revise beliefs as new evidence overturns earlier claims.
Use /bean to open a cross-model blindspot review on high-stakes work.
